title: Crispy Sweet Jamaican Brussels Sprouts Fritters
description: Sweet, crunchy fritters that showcase the bright flavor of Brussels sprouts with Jamaican spices and a hint of molasses.
instructions: Trim stems and core Brussels sprouts, then shred them finely. Pat dry with a towel.
In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, baking powder, ginger, cinnamon, and nutmeg.
Add molasses, lime juice, and beaten egg to the dry mix; stir until a thick batter forms.
Fold in shredded sprouts and chopped cilantro, mixing until evenly coated.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat (about 350°F/177°C).
Drop spoonfuls of the mixture into the hot oil, flatten slightly, and fry until golden brown on both sides, about 3‑4 minutes per side.
Transfer fritters to a paper‑towel‑lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve warm with a squeeze of lime and, if desired, a pinch of sea salt.
Store leftovers in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 days.
Reheat in a skillet or microwave until crisp again.
